context.description: A philosophical nonfiction page argues that human behavior is shaped by biological drives, especially sexual impulse, and then shifts to the central question of how information is accessed and why it matters. It introduces the section "Die Quelle des Lichts" and explains perception as filtered through a machine-like mechanism rather than received directly.
normalization_notes: German nonfiction prose page with a section heading.
Title inferred from the source file name.

rows.paragraph_text: The text argues that people can act against their own interests under biological influence, especially sexual impulse, and that men are particularly vulnerable in this scenario. It then turns to the main question of how information is obtained and why that matters.
Section heading: The Source of Light.
The passage explains perception as mediated by a machine-like process: information is filtered and prepared before it becomes conscious awareness, rather than being received directly.
